name: 2A
notes: East Village old school bar. They play porn on the TV for some reason.

name: 2nd Ave. Deli
notes: Old school Jewish deli with the classic sandwiches and sides

name: 5ive Spice
notes: You wouldn't know it from the name or the exterior, but this place has great vietnamese food, including pho,

name: Algonquin
notes: Decent bar. Good service. Great history.

name: All-Antico Vinaio (Broadway)
notes: Famous big Italian sandwich spot. Way too salty.

name: Angelika Film Center & Cafe
address: 18 W Houston St, New York, NY 10012
notes: Art house theater in The Village. Great location. Reliably have the latest art house movies. Crappy theater.

name: Angelina Paris Bryant Park
notes: Fancy French pastry shop next to Bryant Park. Has all the baked goodies. There's hard-to-spot sitdown restaurant in the back that can be a good escape from the hustle and bustle. Good alternative to LPQ and Ole and Steen and all the zoo-like spots nearby.

name: Arturo's Coal Oven Pizza
address: 106 W Houston St, New York, NY 10012
notes: Down to earth pizza place on Houston. Great location. Decent pizza. Low drama.

name: Babbo
notes: Fancy Batali Italian spot by the Washington Square. Good but expensive. Stick to the pasta.

name: Bagel and Schmear
notes: Has everything on its menu, including bagels. They work quick. People like it, so there are lines.

name: Bagel Boss Murray Hill
notes: Standard bagel place with standard bagel stuff. Fast food, Dunkin kind of setup.

name: Bagel Express 3
notes: Standard bagel breakfast counter. Nice location on the Baruch College patio. You won’t regret it.

name: Bagel Market
notes: Tons of bagel sandwiches. More fast casual

name: Balzen
notes: Turkish stuff. Not bad

name: Banc Cafe
notes: It's a bar with small plates. You can skip it.

name: Bao Bao Cafe (39th Street)
address: 214 W 39th St Retail 101, New York, NY 10018
notes: Simple good bowls and buns and such

name: Bar Bastion
address: 610 Lexington Ave 2nd floor, New York, NY 10022
notes: Classy, small cocktail bar up off the street. Good martinis. But you should probably go to The Grill instead.

name: Bar Centrale
notes: The fancy cocktail bar you want. In a converted townhouse. Nice thing in a touristy zone.

name: Bar Jamon
notes: Tapas off Irving Place. Small. Counters not tables. Gourmet small plates. The kind of place people think of when they think of fancy, romantic New York stuff.

name: Bar Moga
address: 128 W Houston St, New York, NY 10012
notes: Japanesey cocktail bar on the Houston party strip. Not cheap but they only have good stuff.

name: Bar Veloce (East Village)
notes: A good enough edition of a good enough wine bar chain. Decent enough place to stop before or after a movie at the Village East.

name: Bar/code
notes: Just a bar

name: Barclay Rex Pipe Shop
notes: Serious cigar shop, not too pricy, nice place to hang on the sidewalk

name: Barn Joo (Nomad)
notes: Dressed up Korean. Good bowls

name: Barney Brown
notes: Standard quick mix sandwich and salad place for office lunchers

name: Bedford Cheese Shop
notes: Fancy cheese and meat shop near Gramercy Park

name: Beekman Ale House
notes: If there's a better normie pub in Tarrytown, I'd like to see it.

name: Beer Culture
notes: Good microbrew selection near Times Square

name: Best Bagel and Coffee
address: 225 W 35th St A, New York, NY 10001
notes: Old-style breakfast spot near Penn Station that makes its bagels on site. Crispy shells. Fresh. Ungentrified presentation. Worth it.

name: Bhatti
notes: Fancy kabobs. Smokey, spicey vegetable dishes. Good for sitdown or takeout.

name: Billymark's West
notes: Dumpy dive bar

name: Birdland
notes: Jazz dinner theater with jazz dinner theater type stuff. Can book great performers.

name: Bites of Xian 32
notes: Cheaper, easier to access version of Xian Famous Foods.

name: Bites of Xian 38
notes: Cheaper, easier to access version of Xian Famous Foods.

name: Bites of Xian 56
notes: A little dirty and small compared to the other locations

name: Bites of Xian 84
notes: Downstairs edition of the Chinese noodles chain on 10th Street. A little messy and not as tight and tasty as the other branches.

name: Black Seed Bagels (Battery Park)
notes: Good local bagels chain. Food courtin' here

name: Black Seed Bagels (Flatiron)
notes: Service here isn't as good as the other chain locations. Get ready to wait.

name: Black Seed Bagels (Nolita)
notes: Good bagels. Doesn't have the full menu of stuff you find other places.

name: Blue Note
notes: Headliners

name: Bocado
notes: Forgettable UES wine bar. Forget it.

name: Boqueria (Flatiron)
notes: Good pintxos

name: Boqueria (Hells Kitchen)
notes: Standard tapas thing. Loud.

name: Borgo
notes: Another fancy restaurant. But this one is drawing people in my neighborhood so I'm all for it. The focaccia with mortadella is a great appetizer.

name: Botanica
address: 47 E Houston St, New York, NY 10012
notes: A total dump, but it's good for cheap beer during happy hour

name: Boucherie West Village
notes: Terrible service. Overpriced

name: Bravo Pizza (Bryant Park)
notes: Simple good slice

name: Bravo Pizza (Hell's Kitchen)
notes: Forgettable pizza chain output

name: Breads Bakery Kiosk
notes: Good spot to hit at Bryant Park for baked goods and sandwiches. Love the vegan sandwich.

name: Bronze Owl
notes: Dark, brassy cocktail bar off Herald Square.

name: Buenos Aires
notes: Unpretentious but a bit corny. You get a big steak!

name: Buona Notte
notes: Standard touristy Little Italy place

name: Buvette Gastrotheque
notes: Crowded Villagey, Frenchy place where the food is actually good.

name: BXL Cafe
notes: Belgian beers near Times Square. Not horrible, not cheap.

name: C as in Charlie
notes: Korean cooked southern

name: Cafe China
notes: Gentrified take on a Chinese lunch spot. Decent but you can do better in the neighborhood.

name: Cafe Gitane
notes: Place to sit outside and eat in Soho. Good enough

name: Cafe Habana
notes: Cuban cafe

name: Cafe Mogador
address: 101 St Marks Pl, New York, NY 10009
notes: Brunchy middleeastern in the East Village

name: Caledonia
notes: Real Scotch selection. Nice and quiet

name: Campbell Apartment
notes: Oldest school. Small

name: Carnegie Club
address: 156 W 56th St, New York City, NY 10019-3800
notes: Cigars and scotch. Essential

name: Carnegie Pizza
address: 200 W 41st St, New York City, NY 10036
notes: eh

name: Casa de Montecristo Cigar Lounge
notes: A welcome pitstop when walking in Turtle Bay. A good spot to hang when it's empty on a quiet Saturday afternoon. Not so great when it's packed and full of odd characters.

name: Chambers
notes: Good neighborhood but fancy place. Great booze

name: Charlie Bird
notes: Standard trendy fancy restaurant. Right around the corner from Film Forum. Good for a pre-movie meal.

name: Chelsea Bagel (Midtown)
notes: Decent deli counter for bagels

name: Chelsea Square Restaurant
notes: One of the old school NYC diners. Decent but not great.

name: Chi
notes: Standard Americanized Chinese sitdown place. All the generic dishes. Decent place to take a large group and get a bunch of stuff.

name: Chick Inn
notes: Lots and lots of chicken. Pretty good.

name: Cho Dang Gol
notes: Excellent Korean diner spot with all the classic dishes. Wait can be long for a table.

name: Chopt
notes: Skip it

name: Chote Nawab
notes: Nice Indian sitdown

name: Churchill Tavern
notes: Generic British pub. Full of bank dudes

name: Citizens of Gramercy
notes: Decent brunchy place of the Austrailian brekkie style. Not too expensive. Casual.

name: Codex
notes: A little precious in selection but a well stocked, nice bookstore where you will find something you want to read.

name: Comodo
address: 23 Lexington Ave, Freehand Hotel, New York City, NY 10010-3727
notes: Freehand hotel bar. Lots ferns and fancy light fixtures. Good booze and a nice place but not exciting.

name: Corner Slice
address: 600 11th Ave, at 45th Street, New York City, NY 10036-2102
notes: Fancy pizza squares

name: Croton Reservoir Tavern
notes: Generic Midtown bar food for lunch. Not terrible but you don't need to be there.

name: Curry in a Hurry
notes: Easy cafeteria style lunch. Not great but it's there. And a cool upstair dining room.

name: Dag Hammarskjold Plaza
notes: Long quiet promenade between 2nd and 1st Avenues with ample trees and benches. Great spot to stop on a cigar walk.

name: Daily Provisions (Union Square)
notes: Great breakfast sandwich. Fancy baked things

name: Daily Provisions (Upper West Side)
notes: Got that crowded UWS cafe vibe. Same good food in that location.

name: Dear Irving
notes: Gussied up cocktail bar with seating nooks for couples and a brassy bar. Good late night stop in Gramercy.

name: Desi Galli
notes: Tremendous rolls and Indian lunch bits

name: Din Tai Fung
notes: The lux Taiwanese dumpling house streamlined and scaled up into a cavernous Midtown manufacturing center

name: Diner 24
notes: 24/7 kitschy diner. Large portions. Clean. What people expect.

name: Dizzy's Club
notes: Pork cracklins over the mains, which aren't great

name: Dominique Ansel Workshop
notes: A lower-profile outpost of the famous Cronut creator's bakery near Madison Square. Great place to pick something up to eat in the park.

name: Don't Tell Mama
notes: Piano bar on restaurant row

name: Double Down Saloon
notes: Gnarly East Village dive. The kind you're looking for.

name: Drink 33
notes: Basement cave that is a combination cocktail bar and tattoo parlor. Colorful staff.

name: East Village Books
address: 99 St Marks Pl, New York, NY 10009
notes: Grungy basement used book store that delivers what you want: Good books at a good price

name: Eataly (Flatiron)
notes: Too crowded. Not that great.

name: Epistrophy
notes: Affordable compared to the rest of the neighborhood

name: Ess-a-Bagel (Hearld Square)
notes: Standard bagel place very well done. NYers love it. This location is way too crowded. Order ahead on door dash.

name: Ess-a-Bagel (Stuytown)
notes: Crammed into a crappy space. Go to Tal's Bagels nearby instead.

name: Evil Twin Brewing
notes: Pinic table style brewery hangout spot. Good brews, but mainly high ABV odd brews like barleywine. Dogs on leashes. Yuppies with their kids.

name: Film Forum
address: 209 W Houston St, New York, NY 10014
notes: The classic dumpy indie movie theater. Great selection. Not too pricey. The good stuff.

name: Fish Cheeks
notes: Sit outside on Bond Street, which alone is worth it

name: Fish Market
notes: Legitimate dive bar with shockingly good food hidden in the Seaport.

name: Frances Tavern
notes: Old school pub near Wall Street. Very put together.

name: Friedman's (Herald Square)
notes: Gentrified Jewish deli food. You do get a real table and real waiter. Decent for a lunch for two.

name: Friend of a Farmer
notes: Gramercy restaurant with good drinks and the stereotypical farm to table food. Easy table to get and good.

name: Fumo (Kip's Bay)
notes: Italian spot that reliable and a decent delivery option

name: Future Village Farm Smoke
notes: Otherwise generic bodega that boasts a high-quality humidor of cigars. The best place to buy a cigar in my neighborhood.

name: Galanga
notes: Generic Village Thai

name: Gran Via
notes: Unexciting Spanish bar. But nice enough and maybe the best place on Stone Street

name: Grand Central Oyster Bar
notes: Large underground oyster bar beneath Grand Central Terminal. Cool location, but the scene and service is chaotic.

name: Grey
notes: Basic bar

name: H&H Bagels (Kips Bay)
notes: Famous local bagel chain. Clean, nice new outlet. Great schmear. Reliable. Good.

name: Hainan Jones
notes: Singapore style poached and roasted chicken at a stall inside a big food court. Not bad for a healthy, gourmet lunch. Market is pretty whatever.

name: Hamachi Sushi 34
notes: Edible Japanese dinner. Sushi ain't good. Get a bento or rice bowl.

name: Hamburger America
address: 155 W Houston St, New York, NY 10012
notes: The burgers are decent but the long line and crowded dining room suck. Not worth the hassles.

name: Harry's
notes: Fancy downtown steakhouse. Good. You won't regret it. But pricey and there are other options in this same vein.

name: Hawksmoor
notes: Upscale British steakhouse. Everything's good, but it's pricey.

name: Hearth
notes: Great service. Good sitdown place in East Village

name: Hells Kitchenette
notes: Down‑to‑earth bodega with excellent simple deli options.

name: Heritage Grand Bakery
notes: Frenchy coffee shop with the standard pastries. Good food. Crammed inside.

name: Hill Country Barbeque
notes: Quality BBQ next to Madison Square. Not mindblowing, and certainly not cheap, but it's the real thing.

name: Hole in the Wall (Flatiron)
notes: Meat better than fish

name: House of Lasagna
notes: Campy "red sauce" restaurant that's a fun neighborhoody meal

name: Hudson Bar and Books
notes: Decent but incredible cigar book. Good smokes and drinks but the decor and setup is cliche.

name: Hungarian Pastry Shop
notes: Hamentaschen, son

name: Hutch and Waldo (Gramercy)
address: 77 Irving Pl Cafe, New York, NY 10003
notes: Bougie coffee shop with the studied California vibe that New Yorker prefer to call Austrailian. Great location south of Gramercy Park. Good when walking.

name: Il Mulino Prime
notes: A fancy Soho steakhouse that will deliver the meats. Good but not great. Has the upscale WeHo vibes.

name: Istanbul Bay Express
notes: Standard doner lunch option near Times Square

name: Ivory Peacock
notes: Loud club music. Great cheese. Huge gin selection in the back. Speakeasy downstairs.

name: Jack Diamonds
notes: Generic neighborhood bar

name: Jack's Wife Freda (SoHo)
notes: Good place for young people but not me

name: Jazz Gallery
notes: Small jazz venue above NoMad. Great place to see up and coming acts. No food.

name: Jimmy's Corner
address: 140 W 44th St, New York City, NY 10036-4046
notes: True dive. $3 beers in 2023. Old school Times Square

name: Johny's Lunchette
notes: Classic greasy spoon breakfast. Good stop before a Yankee's day game

name: Josie's
notes: Simple East Village dive. Pool. Jukebox. Drinks.

name: Juliana’s
notes: Coal fired pizza place in a great location next to Brooklyn Bridge Park. Good spot for dinner before a sunset walk to the water.

name: Julius'
notes: Cheers for gay guys. Real history.

name: Jumbo Bagels
notes: Standard, unpretentious bagel place. All the basics

name: Junior's [Flatbush]
notes: One of the classic NYC diners. Well worn. Easy. Good enough.

name: Just Salad
notes: Generic lunch salad place

name: Kashmir9
notes: Indian lunch spot with cafeteria service

name: Katz's Delicatessen
address: 205 E Houston St, New York, NY 10002
notes: You know what you're gonna get

name: Keen's Steakhouse
address: 72 West 36th Street, New York City, NY 10018-8025
notes: The classic kitschy old school New York steakhouse. Massive steaks. Old school decor. Not a regular stop but for people who are into this kind of thing it's a Mecca.

name: Kin Ramen
notes: Good Ramen house near the park. Will require that you get there early to be in line ahead of the lunch rush.

name: King Cole Bar
address: Two E 55th St, New York, NY 10022
notes: Old hotel bar with a massive, historic mural. Nice cozy space. Expensive. Drinks are okay but not great.

name: Kings Theater
notes: Beautiful old theater that now serves as a music venue. Big. Has both sitting and standing options.

name: Kips Bay AMC
notes: Dumpy theater. AC works. Bathrooms are dirty. Has IMAX.

name: Kung Fu Kitchen
notes: Not bad. Lots of Chinese options, better than the generic Americanized version. I like the hot pots

name: La Bonbonniere
address: 28 Eighth Ave, New York, NY 10014, United States
notes: Not great. Greasy spoon diner.

name: La Grande Boucherie
notes: Overrprice big French place in the middle of Midtown madness

name: Lamb's Club
notes: Has some old school charm but expensive and they rush you

name: Lan Larb Soho
notes: Good Thai delivery option. Big menu. Everything is good. Actually spicy.

name: LaVista Pizza
notes: Standard NYC pizza spot. Good enough. Has that firmer generic crust you see all over.

name: LB33
notes: Fancy cocktails below street level. You know it's open when the green light is on. Live music on weeknights. Pretty by the book, but it's good.

name: Le Bilboquet
notes: Fancy, old school French place. Cheese and steak were good. This is the white table cloth place you expect it to be.

name: Le Dive
notes: Open air bar at the corner of Ludlow and Canal. Has a rundown Mediterranian style. New Yorky views of the gritty city corner.

name: Leo's Bagels
notes: Great bagel place

name: Leyla
notes: Fancy Turkish stuff. Could pair with Lincoln Center visit

name: Liberty Bagels (Midtown)
address: 260 W 35th St, New York City, NY 10001-2503
notes: Classic bagel options

name: Little Alley
notes: Chinese takeout from Murray Hill. Veggies better than the dumplings. Acceptable but not great.

name: Little Beet (Times Square)
notes: Lunch salad counter that specializes in the trendy diets like Keto

name: Little Branch
notes: The underground West Village cocktail bar you're looking for. Nice intimate table seating for two or four. Standard cocktails that are good. Nothing ridiculous.

name: Little italy Pizza (45th Street)
notes: Decent slice in Midtown. Go to Xian Famous Foods across the street instead, though.

name: Little Italy Pizza (48th Street)
notes: Decent slice near 30 Rock

name: Little Italy Pizza (Times Square)
notes: Bad edition of a decent chain

name: Lobby Bar at the Hotel Chelsea
notes: Cool bar

name: Los Tacos No. 1
notes: Good taco spot, believe it or not, on Times Square

name: Lovely's Old Fashioned
notes: Fancy version of old school burgers. Good

name: Lucia of Gramercy
notes: Fancy slice with oils, honey, basil and extras; a little floppy.

name: Lucky Lyndon's
notes: Simple beer-and-shot bar. Small. Punk music. A little cliche but cheap and does the job.

name: Macao Trading Company
notes: Good bar and sitdown place but not above and beyond the typical very nice spot

name: MacDougal Street Alehouse
notes: Dumpy dive below the street, across from the Comedy Cellar. Passable when not crowded. Has a pool bar and Touchtunes. You can do better, though.

name: Mace
notes: Fancy ass cocktail bar. Expensive

name: Made in New York Pizza (Midtown)
notes: Standard slice

name: Manhatta
address: 28 Liberty St 60th floor, New York, NY 10005
notes: Incredible views from 60th story both north and south. Good place to get a drink.

name: Marea
notes: Fancy spot in a fancy location. The desserts are insane concotions.

name: Margon
notes: Small, hole in the wall Cuban lunch counter. Get the Cubano sandwich

name: Martinez Cigars
notes: Handmade cigars! Not the best quality, but good enough and indie as hell.

name: Martiny's
notes: The pretentious cocktail bar you're looking for.

name: McSorley's Old Ale House
notes: The oldest of the old school. A fun curiousity but not a place you will want to hang all night.

name: Mercado Little Spain
notes: Like Eataly but you want to be there. Incredible booze.

name: Merchants Cigar Bar
notes: Underground cigar bar near the 59th Street Bridge on the eastside. Quiet. Not too pricey. Where Al Sharpton smokes.

name: Metrograph
notes: Nice new independent theater near Canal and Seward Park. Often hosts filmmakers.

name: Michael's New York
notes: Old school power lunch place. You will see somebody powerful, but not famous, from the media elite.

name: Mike's Coffee and Deli
notes: Short order cooks and coffee out of a tiny storefront just off Park Avenue. Open very early. Better than any vendor stall.

name: Miznon (The Hugh)
notes: Good, but not as good at the Times Square location, which has a better staff and buzzy atmosphere. This is more of a food court setup.

name: Miznon (Times Square)
notes: Fast casual lunch spot that's actually good. Israeli food. Kosher. Warm, sticky. Love the stew.

name: Mona's
address: 224 Avenue B Unit 14, New York, NY 10009
notes: The type of East Village divebar you want

name: Moonlight Diner
notes: Meh diner by the tram to Roosevelt Island

name: Morning Side Cafe
address: W 112th St and Manhattan Ave New York, NY 10026
notes: Humble terraced patio cafe with shockingly good food. Crabs in a tin. Great hot dog. Lovely view of the park. Great for a weekend lunch.

name: Murray Hill Diner
notes: The standard NYC diner. Fast, good, everything you expect.

name: Nathan's Famous
notes: The real deal out on the boardwalk

name: Naya (Midtown)
notes: Standard quick mix mediterranian counter place with salads and such

name: New York Pizza Suprema
notes: Great fancy slice. Classic grandma. Love the meatball.

name: Ninth Street Expresso
notes: Decent coffee shop inside the Threes Brewery building

name: Norma
notes: Pizza and pasta place on Kips Bay strip. Good but not great. The standard decent NYC sidewalk restaurant and vibe.

name: NYC Pizza Kitchen
notes: Generic pizza place. You can skip it. I go there because it's on my block.

name: OBEO
notes: Noodles. Fine.

name: Old Town Bar
notes: Old school pub just off Union Square. Food is better than it should be. One of the old NYC bars you want to hit.

name: Osamil
notes: Flavorful Korean gastropub stuff

name: Oscar Wilde
notes: Loud and overblown, kinda fun

name: Osteria Al Doge
notes: Pasta in Times Square. Not terrible but you can skip it.

name: Osteria Carlina
notes: Standard Italian place. Pasta is good. Service is not.

name: Overstory
address: 70 Pine St 64th Floor, New York, NY 10005
notes: Small cocktail bar on the 64th story with sweeping views of the harbor and city. Not cheap.

name: Padi D'NYC
notes: Inside of the Urban Hawker food hall. Malaysian noodles and soups and rendang and all that good stuff. Spicy. Good.

name: Parisien Bistrot
notes: Classic French dinner. Not fancy but good. Tiny. A fun neighborhood place.

name: Park Avenue Tavery
notes: Skip this generic midtown bar.

name: Patzeria Perfect Pizza
notes: Grandma slice is good. Lasanga slice is their special

name: Peacock Alley
notes: The fancy bar and restaurant in the lobby of the renovated Waldorf Astoria. A good way to gawk at the old building's new shine.

name: Peculiar Pub
notes: Dive

name: Peppercorn Station
notes: Real ma la numbing spices on the mapo. Beans app is good.

name: Perros y Vainas en la Plaza
address: Inside Greeley Square Park, 97 W 32nd St, New York, NY 10001
notes: Arepa spot that isn't as good as it should be. You can skip it. Get some Korean food instead.

name: PHD Terrace at Dream Midtown
notes: Dorky sceney rooftop bar that's overprice and kind of dirty

name: Pizza Foto
notes: Forgettable pizza place in Midtown

name: Playwright Irish Pub
notes: Yet another generic midtown Irish bar

name: Pongol
notes: Southern style Indian food, including Thali plates, good place to hang and eat a lot

name: Pop's Pizza
address: 301 W 57th St Ground floor, New York, NY 10019
notes: Good place for a slice near Columbus Circle and the NYRR Run Center

name: Porchlight
notes: Bougie generic bar in West Chelesa money zone. Decent drinks and lots of seating. Pretty bland though.

name: Prince Street Pizza
address: 27 Prince St A, New York, NY 10012
notes: Believe the hype, but skip the line

name: Printer's Alley
notes: Print shop themed sports bar near Times Square. You can do better.

name: Puffy's Tavern
notes: Quiet, quality tavern on the cobblestones in Tribeca

name: Pulau Pinang
notes: Home cooking in Queens

name: Raines Law Room
notes: Underground speakeasy with tight seating, cocktails. Might be a late night romantic spot for some.

name: Randazzo's Clam Bar
notes: Old school red sauce place by the water. Good times will be had by all.

name: Rangoon
notes: Nice neighborhood place. Not cheap

name: Rolo's
notes: Brunchy place in Ridgewood. Great wood-grilled polenta bread, mortadella, bacon. Small plates are what you want to go in on.

name: Romano Pizza
notes: Generic pizza place. Not bad.

name: Rosie's Kosher Pizza
notes: Disorganized and not very good pizza spot. Skip it for better options nearby.

name: Rudy's Bar and Grill
address: 627 9th Ave, Between 44th And 45th, New York City, NY 10036
notes: Dive of dives. Free hotdogs

name: Rum House
notes: Great old school theater district place for cocktails. You've seen it in Birdman.

name: Russ and Daughters (Hudson Yards)
notes: Just as good as the other locations, but a bit boogier.

name: Russ and Daughters (The Cafe)
notes: Menu is more limited than the epic shop. Not as good for a random drop-in. Better for a brunch with a group.

name: Russ and Daughters (The Shop)
address: 179 E Houston St,  New York, NY
notes: Caviar Cream Cheese is a metaphor for life

name: Sahib
notes: Standard Indian restaurant. Has all the stuff you expect.  Food is pretty good. Service ain't great. You can do better for a sitdown Indian option in this neigborhood.

name: Sally's Bar
notes: Dump. Known for its stabbings.

name: Sardi's
notes: Maybe the most famous restaurant at Times Square. Theater district haunt. Good crowd. Lots of space. Decent martini. Do the upstairs bar. A little worn.

name: Sarge’s Delicatessen & Diner
notes: All the classic Jewish deli stuff, plus bagels, plus breakfast plates. It's what you want.

name: Satis Bistro
notes: Nice place to sit outside and food is good

name: Scandinavia House
notes: Nice little restaurant and a cool place to see Nordic movies

name: Schilling
notes: Austrian sitdown in FiDi. Totally fine.

name: Serafina (Upper East Side)
notes: So-so pizza

name: Sergimmo Salumeria
notes: Legit Italian sandwich place in Midtown

name: Shanghai Time
notes: Decent dumplings

name: Shillelagh Pub
notes: Somehow a suburban dive bar is also a private club

name: Socarrat (Chelsea)
notes: Paella and pintxos

name: Stinger
notes: Decent hotel bar. Solid French 75

name: Stone Barns Center for Food and Agriculture
notes: Fancy farm to table food by and for rich people. The chef got cancelled. It's good though.

name: Stout NYC
notes: Crappy bar by the Garden

name: Strand
notes: The epic bookstore. Has it all: New listings, stocked back catalog, good service and the famous carts of cheap books to pick over on the street.

name: Subway Inn
notes: Famous signage and history. A clean, normal but not very memorable sports bar once you're inside.

name: Szechuan Gourmet
notes: Decent Midtown lunch

name: Tacombi (Upper West Side)
notes: Okay Mexican food. The corn in the cup might be the best thing.

name: Tagmo
notes: Good fancy Indian restaurant in the Seaport pedmall

name: Taim
notes: Generic pita and falafal place. Not terrible but you can skip it.

name: Tal Bagels (Stuytown)
notes: The classic normie bagel place you're looking for. All the basics are great, plus they have matzo ball soup, knishes and the special stuff.

name: Tartine
notes: Fun Village bistro

name: Tavern on the Green
notes: Embrace it for what it is. Get the steak sandwich.

name: Tengri Tagh
notes: Uyghur food. Buns are great

name: Teppen Ramen
notes: Unpretentious Japanese lunch spot. Simple. Easy. Has donburry bowls in addition to ramen.

name: Terra Blues
notes: Bleeker Street tourist zone venue that is a reliably blues show. Music is fun but it will be a zoo of drunk and often disinterested people.

name: Terroir
notes: Great drinks selection in Tribeca

name: The Bedford (Stone Street)
notes: Oyster bar type spot out on Stone Street

name: The Century Club
address: 7 West 43rd Street, near Fifth Avenue, Midtown Manhattan, New York City
notes: One of real deal private clubs. Big beautiful library. Classic lunch and bar setup. Good isnt great but who cares

name: The Dutch
notes: Upscale oyster bar setup. Service is so so. Overdone decor.

name: The Grey Dog
notes: Brunchy place. So so. Loud.

name: The Grill
notes: Beautiful mid-century bar in what used to be the Four Seasons. Great place to get a martini.

name: The Herald
notes: Basic bar on Herald Square. Terrible service

name: The Hugh
notes: Bougie food court on the UES. Where the banky people lunch. Good options.

name: The Mansion
notes: A classic Upper East Side New York diner with a huge menu, great location next to the river

name: The Musket Room
notes: Fancy fixed price. Good service. Nice to sit in the back garden.

name: The Portrait Bar
address: 250 Fifth Avenue, New York City, NY 10001
notes: New school take on the old school NYC cocktail bar. Overappointed and a bit snobby, but it's the classic thing folks are looking for. Great for a small group and outtowners who want the fancy drink experience.

name: The River
notes: Stowed away, cozy cocktail bar on the north end of Columbus Park south of Canal at Mulberry

name: The Shakespeare
notes: Traditional pub in a quiet underground nook with wooden fixtures. Real cask ale is the special attraction.

name: The World's Borough Bookshop
notes: The radical-lefty-but-still-nice-and-down-to-earth neighborhood bookstore you're looking for

name: Tibetan Japanese
notes: The name says it all.

name: Tierney's Tavern
notes: Standard suburban pub

name: Tin Building
notes: Fancy food court at the Seaport with great seafood. Great for tourists.

name: Tina's Cuban Cuisine
notes: Busy lunch place on the Eastside. Not terrible. Has most of the standard Cuban sandwiches and rice dishes.

name: Tom and Jerry's
notes: Great bar for divorced white guys to take their eHarmony dates and hear their favorite Strokes deep cut three times a night

name: Tompkins Square Bagels
notes: The bagel place people really want. Lots of fancy ass sandwiches that are good.

name: Top Thai 55
notes: West Village thai spot that has the basics. For young people, and those who want halal.

name: Trailer Park Lounge
notes: The kitschiest dive on Earth. They didn't leave anything on the field.

name: Treadwell Park
address: 301 South End Ave, New York, NY 10280
notes: Decent beer bar for the neighborhood but generic and loud

name: Tsumo
notes: Trendy omakase counter that'll put truffles on your sushi. It's an Instagram trap but it's good. So why not?

name: Turnmill
notes: Standard sports bar. Big. Has all the screens. Decent beers. If you need the game, they will have it.

name: Ulivo
notes: Great Amaro and Euro-liquor selection. Sardinian food and wine that's not your typical red sauce stuff. A neighorhoody spot in a touristy neighborhood. Cool beareded bartender named Jose from Argentina. Big pours.

name: Uluh
address: 152a 2nd Ave, Ste A, New York City, NY 10003
notes: Broad Chinese menu that's good

name: Ulysses Folkhouse
notes: Totally acceptable pub with a horseshoe bar on Stone Street

name: Union Square Cafe
address: 101 E 19th St, New York City, NY 10003-3104
notes: Fancy bar and restaurant of the classic Gramercy style, right off Park Avenue. More seafood than its sister restaurant, Gramercy Tavern.

name: Unregular Pizza
notes: Bougier than the average pizza spot. A little gimmicky. Not bad but not great.

name: Upside Pizza
notes: Good lunch stop that's better than the standard dollar slice

name: Utopia Bagels
notes: A big clean and new version of the classic NYC bagel place. They bake the bagels on premises

name: Van Da
notes: Gourmet Vietnamese plates in the East Village. Legit good

name: Veselka
address: 144 2nd Avenue, at East 9th St., New York City, NY 10003
notes: Classic Ukrainian food. Actually good

name: Vezzo Pizza
notes: Thin crust pizza in neighborhoody vibe

name: Village East by Angelika
notes: Reliable art house theater in the East Village. Not that nice but good enough.

name: Village Vanguard
notes: House brandy, please

name: Vin Sur Vingt Wine Bar (Flatiron)
notes: Great outdoor seating in Broadway pedmall with view of the Flatiron. Go for the location not the service.

name: Vin Sur Vingt Wine Bar (West Village)
notes: Good spot for a drink outside before a show at the Vanguard. Otherwise skip it.

name: Von
notes: Meh cocktail spot

name: Wagamamma (Flatiron)
address: 210 5th Avenue at, W 26th St, New York, NY 10010
notes: Basic bowls

name: Walker's
address: 16 N Moore St, New York, NY 10013
notes: Old school bar for preppies and Long Island types in Tribeca. Not bad. Old and cozy.

name: Waverly Diner
notes: Standard diner in great West Village location

name: Webster Hall
notes: Cozy club with standing room on the floor and a balcony above. Great location near Astor Place.

name: Xi'an Famous Foods (Chelsea)
notes: Handripped noodles, dumplings, cold skin noodles. All great. Great lunch pick.

name: Xi'an Famous Foods (Midtown)
address: 24 W 45th St, New York City, NY 10036-4205
notes: Only go when the weather is nice. There's not much sitting space. But there's a park across the street where you can sit and eat. Handripped noodles, dumplings, cold skin noodles. All great. Great lunch pick.

name: Zabar's
notes: The quintessential Jewish grocer and bagel counter. Professionally ungentrified. A tradition worthy of relish.

name: Zucker's Bagels and Smoked Fish (Bryant Park)
notes: They have all the basic bagel stuff people want. A goto lunch spot for me. Get takeout and eat in Bryant Park

name: Zucker's Bagels and Smoked Fish (FiDi)
notes: The basic bagel stuff. Good but not a great storefront to hang at.

name: Zucker's Bagels and Smoked Fish (Murray Hill)
notes: The basic bagel stuff. Good but not a great storefront to hang at.
